intro:
I couldn't find any information about the specific plant "Dupatya pauciflora." It is possible that it is a misspelling or a lesser-known plant species. However, I found information about the plant "Dichopogon pauciflorus" and "Dysoxylum pauciflorum" which are different plant species.

However, I can provide information about a plant called "Dupatya" or "Dichopogon", which is actually a synonym for "Dichopogon pauciflorus".

Dichopogon pauciflorus, a type of flowering plant, is native to Australia and belongs to the family Asphodelaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

The bioactive constituents of Dichopogon pauciflorus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made from Dichopogon pauciflorus include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
